steps.description: Anneal the as-received substrate to induce segregation of rare-earth elements onto the surface.
Immerse the annealed substrate in a basic solution to etch out the surface layer.
Rinse with deionized water.

synthesis.notes: The wet basic etching method produces smooth atomically flat one-unit cell step-terrace surfaces with predominant B-site (ScO2) termination for rare earth scandates.

Soak substrates in deionized (DI) water in an ultrasonic bath to transform SrO to a hydroxide complex.
Etch the substrate in buffered hydrofluoric acid (BHF) to remove hydroxide complexes.
Anneal the chemically treated substrates to obtain atomically flat surfaces with step-terrace structures.

steps.description: Thermal annealing of the substrate to produce an atomically flat A-site terminated surface.

synthesis.notes: High temperature annealing (up to 1200 °C for 20 h in oxygen flow) is sufficient to produce A-site (NdO) terminated atomically flat smooth surfaces. B-site termination can be achieved if wet chemical etching (BHF or HCl+NH4Cl) is used instead.

steps.description: Clean the substrate with acetone and methanol.
Soak in ultrasonically-stimulated water.
Wet etch with dilute HCl to selectively etch out lanthanum-related oxides.
Anneal to produce an atomically flat surface with step-terrace structure.

synthesis.notes: The procedure results in Al-rich termination (AlO2). Step height is approximately 3.78 Å.
